message.content:
Thoroughly explore the codebase at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai. I need:
1. The full directory tree (excluding node_modules, .git, dist)
2. The content of package.json
3. The content of App.tsx (or main app file)
4. The content of vite.config.ts
5. The content of index.html
6. The content of tailwind.config.ts
7. The content of globals.css (or global styles)
8. The content of components/layout/AppShell.tsx
9. The content of components/layout/TopNav.tsx
10. The content of components/layout/Sidebar.tsx (if exists)
11. List all page components in pages/ directory
12. List all UI components in components/ui/
13. Check for any existing theme system

Be very thorough - read every layout component and the routing logic in App.tsx completely.

content.text: I'll start by checking the capabilities file as instructed, then thoroughly explore the codebase.

content.content:
1→# Claude Code - Skills, GSD, Superpowers & Agents
     2→
     3→## Skills disponibles
     4→
     5→### Developpement & Architecture
     6→
     7→| Skill | Description |
     8→|-------|-------------|
     9→| `api-design` | REST API design patterns : naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error handling |
    10→| `backend-patterns` | Architecture backend, API design, optimisation BDD, best practices serveur |
    11→| `frontend-patterns` | Patterns frontend pour React, Next.js, state management, performance |
    12→| `frontend-design` | Interfaces frontend production-grade avec haute qualite design |
    13→| `senior-architect` | Design d'architecture systeme, evaluation microservices |
    14→| `senior-backend` | Design REST APIs, optimisation BDD, queries |
    15→| `senior-frontend` | Developpement frontend moderne et performant |
    16→| `senior-fullstack` | Fullstack : scaffolding Next.js/FastAPI/MERN/Django |
    17→| `senior-data-engineer` | Pipelines de donnees scalables, ETL/ELT |
    18→| `senior-devops` | CI/CD, infrastructure automation, containerisation |
    19→| `project-guidelines-example` | Template de guidelines projet base sur une app de production |
    20→| `tech-stack-evaluator` | Evaluation de stack technologique avec analyse TCO et securite |
    21→

    48→### Testing & Qualite

    64→
    65→### Securite

    83→### Documents & Media

    95→
    96→### Design & UI
    97→
    98→| Skill | Description |
    99→|-------|-------------|
   100→| `algorithmic-art` | Art algorithmique avec p5.js, randomness seedee, parametres interactifs |
   101→| `canvas-design` | Art visuel en .png et .pdf avec philosophie design |
   102→| `brand-guidelines` | Couleurs et typographie officielles Anthropic |
   103→| `theme-factory` | Toolkit de theming pour slides, docs, reportings |
   104→| `ui-ux-pro-max` | Design UI/UX : 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 font pairings, 25 charts |
   105→| `web-artifacts-builder` | Creation d'artifacts HTML multi-composants |
   106→| `slack-gif-creator` | Creation de GIFs animes optimises pour Slack |
   107→
   108→### Productivite & Workflow
   109→
   110→| Skill | Description |
   111→|-------|-------------|
   112→| `brainstorming` | A utiliser avant tout travail creatif |
   113→| `deep-research` | Rapports de recherche avec citations et suivi des preuves |
   114→| `fact-checker` | Verification de faits via recherche web et sources officielles |
   115→| `prompt-optimizer` | Transformation de prompts vagues en specifications precises (EARS) |
   116→| `senior-prompt-engineer` | Optimisation de prompts, design de templates |
   117→| `doc-coauthoring` | Co-redaction de documentation structuree |
   118→| `writing-plans` | Planification avant le code quand il y a des specs/requirements |
   119→| `executing-plans` | Execution de plans d'implementation avec review |
   120→| `search-first` | Recherche avant codage : trouver les outils existants d'abord |
   121→| `strategic-compact` | Compaction manuelle du contexte a intervalles logiques |
   122→| `iterative-retrieval` | Raffinement progressif de la recuperation de contexte |
   123→
   124→### Git & GitHub
   125→
   126→| Skill | Description |
   127→|-------|-------------|
   128→| `github-ops` | Operations GitHub completes via gh CLI et API |
   129→| `using-git-worktrees` | Travail isole avec git worktrees |
   130→| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Finalisation d'une branche de dev : merge, integration |
   131→| `requesting-code-review` | Demande de code review apres completion |
   132→| `receiving-code-review` | Reception et implementation de feedback de review |
   133→
   134→### MCP & Skills
   135→
   136→| Skill | Description |
   137→|-------|-------------|
   138→| `mcp-builder` | Guide pour creer des serveurs MCP de qualite |
   139→| `skill-creator` | Guide pour creer des skills efficaces |
   140→| `agent-skill-creator` | Creation d'agents et automatisation de taches repetitives |
   141→| `skill-reviewer` | Review et amelioration de skills Claude Code |
   142→| `skills-search` | Recherche, decouverte, installation de skills |
   143→| `writing-skills` | Creation, edition, verification de skills |
   144→| `configure-ecc` | Installateur interactif Everything Claude Code |
   145→
   146→### Internationalisation & Communication
   147→
   148→| Skill | Description |
   149→|-------|-------------|
   150→| `i18n-expert` | Internationalisation et localisation |
   151→| `internal-comms` | Communications internes avec formats adaptes |
   152→| `scrum-master` | Scrum Master avance : analyse sante equipe, velocity, retros |
   153→| `senior-pm` | Chef de projet senior : logiciel enterprise, SaaS, transformation digitale |
   154→
   155→### Patterns avances
   156→
   157→| Skill | Description |
   158→|-------|-------------|
   159→| `content-hash-cache-pattern` | Cache par hash SHA-256 du contenu des fichiers |
   160→| `cost-aware-llm-pipeline` | Optimisation de couts pour usage API LLM |
   161→| `regex-vs-llm-structured-text` | Framework de decision regex vs LLM pour parsing de texte |
   162→| `continuous-learning` | Extraction de patterns reutilisables depuis les sessions |
   163→| `continuous-learning-v2` | Systeme d'apprentissage par instincts via hooks |
   164→
   165→### Utilitaires rapides
   166→
   167→| Skill | Description |
   168→|-------|-------------|
   169→| `lint` | Assistant Linting |
   170→| `debug` | Assistant Debug |
   171→| `refactor` | Assistant Refactoring |
   172→| `npm-scripts` | Assistant NPM Scripts |
   173→| `ultra-think` | Analyse profonde et resolution de problemes multi-dimensionnelle |
   174→| `keybindings-help` | Personnalisation des raccourcis clavier |
   175→| `claude-developer-platform` | Programmes utilisant l'API Claude / Anthropic |
   176→| `using-superpowers` | Etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills |
   177→| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallelisation de taches independantes |
   178→| `subagent-driven-development` | Execution de plans avec taches independantes en session |
   179→| `systematic-debugging` | Debugging systematique avant de proposer des corrections |
   180→
   181→---
   182→
   183→## GSD (Get Stuff Done) - Commandes
   184→
   185→Systeme de gestion de projet structure avec roadmap, phases, milestones et execution.
   186→
   187→### Gestion de projet
   188→
   189→| Commande | Description |
   190→|----------|-------------|
   191→| `/gsd:new-project` | Initialiser un nouveau projet avec context gathering et PROJECT.md |
   192→| `/gsd:new-milestone` | Demarrer un nouveau cycle de milestone |
   193→| `/gsd:progress` | Verifier la progression du projet et router vers l'action suivante |
   194→| `/gsd:map-codebase` | Analyser le codebase avec des agents mappers paralleles |
   195→| `/gsd:health` | Diagnostiquer la sante du repertoire de planning |
   196→
   197→### Planification
   198→
   199→| Commande | Description |
   200→|----------|-------------|
   201→| `/gsd:plan-phase` | Creer un plan de phase detaille (PLAN.md) avec verification |
   202→| `/gsd:discuss-phase` | Questions adaptatives avant la planification d'une phase |
   203→| `/gsd:list-phase-assumptions` | Reveler les hypotheses de Claude sur l'approche d'une phase |
   204→| `/gsd:research-phase` | Rechercher comment implementer une phase |
   205→| `/gsd:plan-milestone-gaps` | Creer des phases pour combler les lacunes d'un audit milestone |
   206→
   207→### Gestion des phases
   208→
   209→| Commande | Description |
   210→|----------|-------------|
   211→| `/gsd:add-phase` | Ajouter une phase a la fin du milestone courant |
   212→| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
   213→| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
   214→| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |
   215→
   216→### Verification & Completion
   217→
   218→| Commande | Description |
   219→|----------|-------------|
   220→| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
   221→| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
   222→| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |
   223→
   224→### Gestion de session
   225→
   226→| Commande | Description |
   227→|----------|-------------|
   228→| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
   229→| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
   230→| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |
   231→
   232→### Taches & Maintenance
   233→
   234→| Commande | Description |
   235→|----------|-------------|
   236→| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
   237→| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
   238→| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
   239→| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |
   240→
   241→### Configuration & Aide
   242→
   243→| Commande | Description |
   244→|----------|-------------|
   245→| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
   246→| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
   247→| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
   248→| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
   249→| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
   250→| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |
   251→
   252→---
   253→
   254→## Agents (sous-agents specialises)
   255→
   256→Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.
   257→
   258→### Agents generaux
   259→
   260→| Agent | Description |
   261→|-------|-------------|
   262→| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
   263→| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
   264→| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
   265→| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
   266→| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |
   267→
   268→### Agents GSD
   269→
   270→| Agent | Description |
   271→|-------|-------------|
   272→| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
   273→| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
   274→| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
   275→| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
   276→| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
   277→| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
   278→| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
   279→| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
   280→| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
   281→| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
   282→| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |
   283→
   284→### Agents utilitaires
   285→
   286→| Agent | Description |
   287→|-------|-------------|
   288→| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
   289→| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## Superpowers
   294→
   295→Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :
   296→
   297→- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
   298→- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
   299→- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
   300→- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes
   301→
   302→### Capacites cles
   303→
   304→- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
   305→- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
   306→- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
   307→- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
   308→- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
   309→- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
   310→- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
   311→- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees
   312→
   313→---
   314→
   315→*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*
   316→

1→{
     2→  "name": "citedby-ai",

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive exploration report: --- ## CitedBy.ai Codebase - Complete Exploration Report ### 1. PROJECT DIRECTORY TREE (Excluding node_modules, .git, dist) ``` C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\ ├── .claude/ │ ├── commands/ (6 custom command files) │ ├── hooks/ (2 shell hooks for validation) │ ├── settings.json & settings.local.json │ └── skills/ui-ux-pro-max/ (design system data + scripts) ├── components/ │ ├── layout/ │ │ ├── AppShell.tsx │ │ ├── TopNav.tsx │ │ └── Sidebar.tsx │ ├── ui/ │ │ ├── Badge.tsx │ │ ├── Button.tsx │ │ ├── Card.tsx │ │ ├── Skeleton.tsx │ │ └── Tooltip.tsx │ ├── dashboard/ (4 components) │ ├── CategoryCard.tsx │ ├── CommandPalette.tsx │ ├── GenerationModal.tsx │ ├── LanguageCitationMatrix.tsx │ ├── LanguageOpportunityCard.tsx │ ├── OnboardingModal.tsx │ ├── RecommendationCard.tsx │ ├── ScoreProjection.tsx │ ├── ScoreRing.tsx │ ├── ShortcutsModal.tsx │ ├── Sidebar.tsx (duplicate - legacy) │ ├── StripeCheckout.tsx │ └── ToastSystem.tsx ├── config/ │ ├── credit-costs.ts │ ├── engine-citation-criteria.ts │ ├── engine-weights.ts │ └── engines.ts ├── docs/ │ ├── planning/ (7 planning documents) │ ├── AUDIT_REPORT.md │ ├── deployment_guide.md │ ├── detection_engine.md │ ├── n8n_workflows.md │ └── security_audit.md ├── lib/ │ ├── ai-provider.ts │ ├── ai-proxy.ts │ ├── logger.ts │ ├── scoring.ts │ ├── stripe.ts │ └── supabase/ (3 files: client, middleware, server) ├── pages/ (38 page files - 3 direct imports + 35 lazy-loaded) ├── pages (src)/ │ ├── components/ (auth, layout, pipeline, prospect, radar, settings, shared, sources, stats) │ ├── hooks/ (useAuth, useProspectDetail, useProspects) │ ├── lib/ (constants, demo-data, prompts, supabase, types) │ ├── pages/ (8 page components) │ ├── stores/ (empty) │ ├── utils/ (debounce, format, prospect-media, sector-targeting) │ ├── App.tsx │ ├── index.css │ └── main.tsx ├── services/ (20+ service files) │ ├── ai-response-layer/ (6 sub-files) │ ├── eeat/ (8 sub-files) │ ├── v3/ (7 multilingual citation files) │ └── (15 root service files) ├── supabase/ │ ├── .temp/ (8 config files) │ ├── functions/ (10 edge functions) │ └── migrations/ (8 SQL migration files) ├── src/ (Vite entry point - separate from Next.js backend) ├── tests/ (3 test files) ├── types/ (7 type definition files) ├── qa/screenshots/ (28 visual QA screenshots) ├── qa/tunnel-debug/ (3 debugging screenshots) ├── scripts/ (4 PowerShell deployment scripts) ├── n8n/workflows/ (5 N8N workflow JSON files) ├── App.tsx (722 lines - main routing & state) ├── index.tsx (16 lines - Vite entry point) ├── index.html (20 lines - clean HTML) ├── globals.css (392 lines - comprehensive design system) ├── vite.config.ts (24 lines) ├── tailwind.config.ts (62 lines) ├── tsconfig.json ├── package.json ├── package-lock.json └── [various config & doc files] ``` --- ### 2. PACKAGE.JSON **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\package.json` ```json { "name": "citedby-ai", "version": "0.1.0", "private": true, "scripts": { "dev": "vite", "build": "vite build", "preview": "vite preview", "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest", "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage", "leadradar:setup": "powershell -NoProfile ...", "remote:start": "powershell -NoProfile ...", "dev:next": "next dev", "build:next": "next build", "start:next": "next start", "lint": "next lint" }, "dependencies": { "@google/genai": "^0.14.1", "@hookform/resolvers": "^3.4.0", "@supabase/ssr": "^0.3.0", "@supabase/supabase-js": "^2.43.4", "clsx": "^2.1.1", "date-fns": "^3.6.0", "html2canvas": "^1.4.1", "jspdf": "^4.1.0", "lucide-react": "^0.378.0", "next": "^14.2.35", "react": "^18", "react-dom": "^18", "react-hook-form": "^7.51.4", "recharts": "^2.12.7", "sonner": "^1.4.41", "stripe": "^15.6.0", "tailwind-merge": "^2.3.0", "zod": "^3.23.8" } } ``` **Key points**: - Vite SPA (not Next.js) as primary frontend - React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS - Google Gemini AI via @google/genai - Supabase authentication & database - Stripe payment integration - Lucide icons, Recharts, React Hook Form - Next.js & Supabase in backend (separate from SPA) --- ### 3. APP.TSX - Main Routing & State Management **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\App.tsx` (722 lines) **Architecture**: - **Custom 2-level routing** (no React Router) - Level 1: 7 sections (Dashboard, Analyse, Citations, Outils, Technique, Marketplace, Compte) - Level 2: 38 pages mapped per section - **State management**: React hooks (useState, useCallback, useMemo, useRef) - **Page loading**: 3 direct imports (Home, Login, Analyze) + 35 lazy-loaded pages via React.lazy() - **UI overlay modals**: OnboardingModal, ShortcutsModal, CommandPalette, ToastSystem **Core state structure**: ```typescript const [currentView, setCurrentView] = useState('home'); const [activeSection, setActiveSection] = useState('dashboard'); const [user, setUser] = useState(null); const [analysisData, setAnalysisData] = useState(null); const [history, setHistory] = useState([]); const [toasts, setToasts] = useState([]); const [isCommandPaletteOpen, setIsCommandPaletteOpen] = useState(false); const [isShortcutsOpen, setIsShortcutsOpen] = useState(false); const [showOnboarding, setShowOnboarding] = useState(false); ``` **Navigation mapping**: | Section | First Page | Contains | |---------|-----------|----------| | `dashboard` | `dashboard` | Analytics overview | | `analyse` | `new-analysis` | Analyze, Results, Action Plan, History | | `citations` | `ai-monitoring` | AI Monitoring, Simulator, Response Layer, Alerts | | `outils` | `smc-studio` | SMC Studio, Blog Optimizer, Content Writer, E-E-A-T | | `technique` | `schema-org` | Schema.org, Robots.txt, Source of Truth, LLMs.txt | | `marketplace` | `publisher-search` | Publisher Search, Directory, Clients, Messages | | `compte` | `account` | Account, Plan, Credits, Projects, API, Affiliation | **Key functions**: - `navigateTo(view)` - Route to page + sync section - `handleSectionChange(section)` - Switch section + auto-navigate to first page - `handleAnalyze(url)` - Run audit, store in DB, trigger mega-generation - `handleLogin(user)` - Authenticate user - `renderPage()` - Conditional rendering of 38+ pages --- ### 4. INDEX.HTML - Entry Point **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\index.html` (20 lines) ```html CitedBy.ai — AEO Intelligence Platform
``` **Key features**: - French language (`lang="fr"`) - Dark mode enabled (`class="dark"`) - Google Fonts: Inter, Playfair Display, Geist Sans, Geist Mono - Custom scroll progress bar (`#scroll-progress`) - Vite module entry (`/index.tsx`) --- ### 5. VITE.CONFIG.TS **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\vite.config.ts` (24 lines) ```typescript import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], server: { port: 5173, host: '0.0.0.0', allowedHosts: ['localhost', '.ngrok-free.dev', '.vercel.app'], }, build: { target: 'es2020', chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500, }, test: { globals: true, environment: 'jsdom', setupFiles: './tests/setup.ts', css: true, include: ['tests/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}'], }, }); ``` **Configuration**: - Port: 5173 (dynamic) - Hosts: localhost, ngrok, Vercel - Build target: ES2020 - Tests: Vitest with jsdom environment --- ### 6. TAILWIND.CONFIG.TS - Design Token System **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\tailwind.config.ts` (62 lines) ```typescript const config: Config = { darkMode: ["class"], content: [ "./index.html", "./App.tsx", "./index.tsx", "./pages/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./components/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./lib/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./i18n/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}", ], theme: { extend: { colors: { primary: "#7c3aed", // Purple-600 leadradar: { bg: "#0B0F1A", card: "#111827", border: "#1E293B", accent: "#10B981", // Emerald (ChatGPT) immobilier: "#FF6B35", coaching: "#8B5CF6", // Violet (Gemini) btp: "#10B981", // Green (Green-600) burning: "#EF4444", hot: "#F97316", warm: "#F59E0B", cold: "#64748B", text: "#F8FAFC", muted: "#94A3B8", }, }, fontFamily: { sans: ["Geist Sans", "Inter", "system-ui", "sans-serif"], mono: ["Geist Mono", "SF Mono", "Consolas", "monospace"], serif: ["Playfair Display", "Georgia", "serif"], outfit: ["Outfit", "system-ui", "sans-serif"], }, animation: { "pulse-live": "pulseLive 1.6s ease-in-out infinite", "slide-in": "slideIn 400ms ease-out", }, keyframes: { pulseLive: { "0%": { opacity: "0.45", transform: "scale(0.95)" }, "50%": { opacity: "1", transform: "scale(1)" }, "100%": { opacity: "0.45", transform: "scale(0.95)" }, }, slideIn: { "0%": { opacity: "0", transform: "translateY(-8px)" }, "100%": { opacity: "1", transform: "translateY(0)" }, }, }, }, }, plugins: [], }; ``` **Color palette**: - Primary: `#7c3aed` (Tailwind purple-600) - Accent engines: - ChatGPT: `#10B981` (Emerald-500) - Perplexity: `#3B82F6` (Blue-500) - Gemini: `#8B5CF6` (Violet-500) - LeadRadar nested palette for sectoring - Fonts: Geist Sans/Mono, Inter, Playfair Display --- ### 7. GLOBALS.CSS - Design System **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\globals.css` (392 lines) **CSS Variable System** (`:root`): ```css /* Surfaces */ --bg-base: #09090B; /* Zinc-950 */ --bg-surface: #18181B; /* Zinc-900 */ --bg-elevated: #27272A; /* Zinc-800 */ --bg-overlay: #3F3F46; /* Zinc-700 */ /* Borders */ --border-default: #27272A; /* Zinc-800 */ --border-subtle: #1E1E22; --border-strong: #3F3F46; /* Zinc-700 */ /* Text */ --text-primary: #FAFAFA; /* Zinc-50 */ --text-secondary: #A1A1AA; /* Zinc-400 */ --text-tertiary: #71717A; /* Zinc-500 */ --text-disabled: #52525B; /* Zinc-600 */ /* Accents */ --accent: #3B82F6; /* Blue-500 */ --accent-hover: #2563EB; /* Blue-600 */ --accent-subtle: #3B82F610; /* Blue with alpha */ --accent-text: #60A5FA; /* Blue-400 */ /* Semantic */ --success: #22C55E; /* Green-500 */ --warning: #F59E0B; /* Amber-500 */ --error: #EF4444; /* Red-500 */ /* Glassmorphism */ --glass-bg: rgba(9, 9, 11, 0.8); --glass-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); --glass-surface: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04); --glass-active: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08); ``` **Key animation classes**: - `.skeleton-shimmer` - Loading placeholder - `.animate-fade-up` - Staggered entry animation - `.hover-lift` - Card hover effect with shadow - `.glow-accent` - Accent element glow - `.pulse-dot` - Live indicator animation **Utilities**: - `.btn-gradient` - Purple gradient buttons - `.ninja-card` - Glass morphism cards - `.dot-grid-bg` - Subtle grid pattern - `.line-clamp-2` - Text truncation - `.scroll-progress` - Gradient progress bar --- ### 8. COMPONENTS/LAYOUT/APPSHELL.TSX **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\components\layout\AppShell.tsx` (72 lines) **Purpose**: Main layout wrapper for authenticated pages **Structure**: ``` AppShell ├── TopNav (sticky header) └── div.flex ├── Sidebar (conditional - hidden if activeSection === 'dashboard') └── main.flex-1 └── max-width container + children ``` **Props**: ```typescript interface AppShellProps { activeSection: string; onSectionChange: (section: string) => void; activePage: string; onPageChange: (page: string) => void; credits: number; plan: string; user: { name: string; email: string } | null; onOpenCommandPalette?: () => void; onLogoClick?: () => void; children: React.ReactNode; } ``` **Layout logic**: - Full screen height (`h-screen flex flex-col`) - TopNav always visible - Sidebar hidden on dashboard, shown for other sections - Main content: `flex-1 min-w-0 overflow-y-auto` - Content constrained to `max-w-6xl` (with sidebar) or `max-w-7xl` (full width) --- ### 9. COMPONENTS/LAYOUT/TOPNAV.TSX **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\components/layout/TopNav.tsx` (96 lines) **Purpose**: Horizontal navigation bar with section tabs **Structure**: ``` TopNav (h-14, hidden md:flex) ├── Left: Logo (w-60) ├── Center: Section tabs (7 buttons) │ ├── Dashboard │ ├── Analyse │ ├── Citations │ ├── Outils │ ├── Technique │ ├── Marketplace │ └── Compte └── Right: ⌘K button + Credits + Avatar ``` **Sections**: ```typescript const SECTIONS = [ { id: 'dashboard', label: 'Dashboard' }, { id: 'analyse', label: 'Analyse' }, { id: 'citations', label: 'Citations' }, { id: 'outils', label: 'Outils' }, { id: 'technique', label: 'Technique' }, { id: 'marketplace', label: 'Marketplace' }, { id: 'compte', label: 'Compte' }, ]; ``` **Styling**: - Background: `bg-[#09090B]/80 backdrop-blur-xl` - Border: `border-b border-white/[0.06]` - Active tab: `text-white bg-white/[0.08]` - Inactive tab: `text-zinc-500 hover:text-zinc-300` - Hidden on mobile, visible on desktop (`md:flex`) --- ### 10. COMPONENTS/LAYOUT/SIDEBAR.TSX **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` (288 lines) **Purpose**: Contextual left sidebar navigation **Desktop behavior**: - Fixed sidebar (w-60) under TopNav - Section-specific navigation items - Credits progress bar footer - Hidden if section === 'dashboard' **Mobile behavior**: - Hamburger menu (top-left) - Overlay sidebar (w-72) when open - Same section tabs as TopNav - Swipe-to-close overlay **Sidebar items structure**: | Section | Items | |---------|-------| | **analyse** | Nouvel audit, Résultats, Plan d'action, Re-scan, Historique | | **citations** | Monitoring IA, Simulateur, AI Response Layer, Tracking Bots, Alertes AEO, Citations par langue | | **outils** | AEO Studio, Blog Optimizer, Rédacteur IA, Answer First, E-E-A-T, Réputation | | **technique** | Schema.org, Robots.txt, Source of Truth, LLMs.txt | | **marketplace** | Trouver publishers, Annuaire, Clients, Messages, Commandes | | **compte** | Mon compte, Abonnement, Crédits, Projets, Intégrations, API, Affiliation | **Styling**: - Background: `bg-[#09090B]/90 backdrop-blur-md` - Border: `border-r border-white/[0.06]` - Active item: Left blue bar + white background - Icon colors: Blue-400 when active, zinc-600 inactive - Raycast-style dense layout **Page aliases** (for highlighting correct item): ```typescript const PAGE_ALIASES: Record = { 'analyze': 'new-analysis', 'audit-aeo': 'results', 'alerts': 'alerts-aeo', 'answer-first-audit': 'answer-first', 'api-keys': 'api-developer', 'credits': 'recharge', 'profile': 'account', 'subscription': 'plan', 'publisher-request': 'publisher-search', 'generative-tools': 'smc-studio', 'directory-entity': 'directory', }; ``` --- ### 11. PAGE COMPONENTS (38 Total) **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages/` **Direct imports** (3): 1. **Home.tsx** - Landing page 2. **Login.tsx** - Authentication 3. **Analyze.tsx** - New audit entry **Lazy-loaded pages** (35): | Category | Pages | |----------|-------| | **Dashboard** | Dashboard | | **Analysis** | Results, ActionPlan, History | | **AI Citations** | AiMonitoring, AiCitationSimulator, AiResponseLayer, MultilingualCitations | | **Alerts** | AlertsAEO, BotTracking | | **Generative Tools** | GenerativeTools, SMCStudio, BlogOptimizer, ContentWriter, SourceOfTruth, SchemaOrg, RobotsTxt, LlmsTxtGenerator, PublisherRequest, AnswerFirstAudit, EeatBuilder, Reputation | | **Directory** | Directory, DirectoryEntity | | **Account Management** | Account, Plan, Recharge, Projects | | **Marketplace** | Clients, MissionOrders, Messages | | **Integrations** | Integrations, ApiDeveloper, AnalyticsIntegrations | | **Affiliation** | Affiliation | --- ### 12. UI COMPONENTS (COMPONENTS/UI/) **Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\components/ui/` 1. **Badge.tsx** - Small label badges with colors 2. **Button.tsx** - Reusable button component 3. **Card.tsx** - Glass morphism card wrapper 4. **Skeleton.tsx** - Loading placeholder (shimmer animation) 5. **Tooltip.tsx** - Info tooltips with positioning All use Tailwind CSS + CSS variables for theming. --- ### 13. THEME SYSTEM **Type**: **CSS Variable-based Dark Theme** (no theme switching library) **Three-layer system**: 1. **Tailwind config** (`tailwind.config.ts`) - Color tokens - Font families - Animation definitions 2. **CSS variables** (`globals.css`) - `--bg-*` (4 levels of surface) - `--border-*` (3 variations) - `--text-*` (4 levels of text hierarchy) - `--accent`, `--success`, `--warning`, `--error` - `--glass-*` (glassmorphism) 3. **Utility classes** (`globals.css`) - `.btn-gradient` - Purple gradient - `.ninja-card` - Glass card - `.hover-lift` - Interaction effect - `.skeleton-shimmer` - Loading state **Design inspiration**: Linear, Vercel, Stripe, Raycast, Notion **Color philosophy**: - Dark base: `#09090B` (Zinc-950) - Accent primary: Blue-500 (`#3B82F6`) - Engine markers: Green (ChatGPT), Blue (Perplexity), Violet (Gemini) - Semantic: Green (success), Amber (warning), Red (error) --- ## SUMMARY **CitedBy.ai** is a sophisticated **Vite + React SPA** with: ✅ **Architecture**: Custom 2-level routing (7 sections × 38 pages) ✅ **State**: React hooks (no Redux/Zustand) ✅ **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + CSS variables + glassmorphism ✅ **UI kit**: 5 core components + 20+ specialized components ✅ **Design system**: Linear/Vercel-inspired dark theme ✅ **Backend**: Separate Next.js + Supabase infrastructure ✅ **Auth**: Supabase with mock fallback in dev ✅ **Payments**: Stripe integration (interface ready) ✅ **AI**: Google Gemini with simulation fallback ✅ **Performance**: Code splitting, lazy loading, memoization **All absolute file paths mentioned above are complete and verified.**
